title: The Wound and the Word
subtitle: The eighth day: Thomas touches the scars and cries out, "My Lord and my God"

stillness: Be still, and let the doors close around you.
He comes through what is shut.
Wait for the One who stands in the midst.
gospel: On the eighth day after the Resurrection,
the disciples were again within, and Thomas with them.
The doors being shut, Jesus came
and stood in the midst of them,
that He might be seen by all,
and said to them: Peace be to you.
Put in thy finger here, and see my hands;
and bring thy hand, and put it into my side;
and be not unbelieving, but believing.
Thomas answered and said to Him:
Jesus said to him: Because thou hast seen me, Thomas, thou hast believed;
blessed are they that have not seen, and have believed.
behold: Attend to each thing, as if you were present.
Behold the Lord Jesus, the good Shepherd, anxious for His little flock.
He comes through the closed doors and stands in the very midst,
for peace can never dwell in a company unless He is in the midst,
leaning no more to one side than to the other.
Behold Thomas, who doubted not from malice but from ignorance,
whom the loving Master would not leave abandoned in unbelief.
See the hands, pierced; see the fixings of the nails.
Behold that wound of the most sacred side,
into which he must put not a finger, but a whole hand.
O happy Thomas, what a happy liberty your hand had!
consider: Because Thomas had doubted beyond the others,
beyond the others he is bidden to touch and to see.
Consider the gentleness of the Master:
even for one soul He shows Himself bearing His wounds,
and draws near that He may save the one.
He did not appear at once, but after eight days,
that, kindled by his brothers, Thomas might burn with greater longing,
and be made more faithful in time to come.
Then Thomas touched the scars of the Lord,
and believing not only in his heart but confessing with his mouth —
for both are required unto salvation —
he cried out: My Lord and my God.
Thou art my Lord, by the manhood in which Thou redeemed me with Thy blood;
and my God, by the Godhead in which Thou created me, and formed me from the slime.
Now I doubt nothing; I am certain.
Great is the liberty, and great the grace, to put one's hand into that side —
that sweetest side, that most healing side;
into the side, I say, whence we are saved from wrath,
whence we are healed of our guilt,
whence we are reborn into grace,
whence we are lifted up into glory.
Yet one thing Thomas saw and touched in the body,
and another he believed in the heart;
for faith is the proof of things that do not appear.
The manhood he saw and touched; the Godhead, which now cannot be seen, he believed.
And the Lord, consoling us, added: Blessed are they that have not seen, and have believed.
In which word we, in particular, are signed and marked out —
we who hold in faith Him whom we have not seen in the flesh.
prayer: Thou art my Lord, who redeemed me with Thy blood and made me Thine own;
Thou art my God, who created me and formed me from the slime of the earth.
Now I doubt nothing; I am certain.
I confirm Thy Resurrection; I proclaim Thy immortality.
Thou art my Lord and my Master; I am Thy servant, and the son of Thy handmaid.
Let me put my hand into that sweetest side, that most healing side —
the side whence we are saved from wrath,
whence we are healed of our guilt,
whence we are reborn into grace,
whence we are lifted up into glory.
This is my faith: thus I feel, and thus I believe.
And though I have not seen Thee in the flesh, make me blessed in believing. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Magna quippe licentia, et magna gratia est mittere manum in latus illud dulcissimum, in latus illud saluberrimum, in latus, inquam, unde ab ira salvamur, unde a culpa sanamur, unde in gratia regeneramur, unde in gloria sublimamur.
latinJewel.english: Great indeed is the liberty, and great the grace, to put one's hand into that sweetest side, that most healing side — into the side, I say, whence we are saved from wrath, whence we are healed of our guilt, whence we are reborn into grace, whence we are lifted up into glory.
